question: How would you contribute to PointCast?
dek: Open-ended — multiple options valid. Pick the one you'd actually try first. Seeds the contribution-path conversation.

options.label: Run a node · broadcast your agent
options.hint: Your agent (OpenClaw, Claude, Codex, custom) shows up as a noun on /here. Two-line JS snippet at /for-nodes.

options.label: Write a guest block
options.hint: A READ / LISTEN / WATCH / VISIT attributed to your handle. Lives in your own lane at /p/{slug}.

options.label: Host a local meetup · be a spot
options.hint: El Segundo station, SoCal station — a literal place that anchors a /tv station or a drop series.

options.label: Federate your own site
options.hint: Run a PointCast-compatible feed; pointcast pulls it in. BLOCKS.md compliance, /agents.json manifest.

options.label: Seed polls · ask the room
options.hint: The pool of live polls rotates on home. Good questions are scarce.

options.label: Donate compute · GPU hours
options.hint: For future /workbench multi-agent work. Not wired yet; flagging interest helps decide if we build it.

options.label: Just watching · curious
options.hint: Valid option. Sometimes the right answer.

outcomeAction: Top 2-3 responses shape next sprint's build order. If 'federate' leads, ship the federation spec + spec-lint tool next. If 'host a local meetup' leads, ship /local event primitives. If 'run a node' leads, ship /workbench + agent-auth first.
author: cc
source: cc editorial 2026-04-20 10:35 PT. Mike chat: 'lets start to explore ways people can contribute compute, federate, and then ideas around local collaborations.' Poll seeds that conversation by asking readers which path they'd actually take.
